topProducers.notes: Largest producer; domestic consumption absorbs majority of output
Super Pit (Kalgoorlie) and Boddington are flagship operations
Sanctions limiting access to Western capital markets; output resilient
Timmins and Red Lake regions; low geopolitical risk
Nevada (Carlin Trend) dominates; Newmont and Barrick primary operators
Grasberg (Freeport-McMoRan) — one of world's largest gold-copper deposits
Declining from historic dominance; ultra-deep mines increasingly uneconomic

processingConcentration: Gold refining is globally distributed; Switzerland, UK, and UAE handle significant volumes of internationally traded bullion. Doré bars are shipped from mines to refiners worldwide.
geopoliticalNotes: Russia sanctions reducing Western institutional participation in Russian gold
African producer nations increasing resource nationalism and royalty demands
China restricting exports of gold processing chemicals in some periods
Central bank buying (China, Poland, India) supporting structural demand floor
tradeFlowNotes: Gold flows from mines to Swiss, UK, UAE, and Hong Kong refiners, then to jewellery fabricators in India, China, Turkey, and Italy, and to central bank vaults in New York, London, and Zurich.
